- `onLook`: (id) => void — Fires when a plaque is faced and activated.
- `depth / height`: number (default 260 / 290) — Room depth and stage height in px.

Notes:
- Pointer-look is gated to fine pointers; on touch the focused stage looks around with the arrow keys.
- Plaques are real buttons — focusing one glides the room to face it, Enter logs the look.
- Under reduced motion the room collapses to a flat, wall-grouped list of focusable plaques with no 3D.

### Camera Rail (KQ-088) — `camera-rail`
Ride the rail; a station snaps to the platform.

A content monorail that rides past a fixed camera — cars recede left and right of the platform, a drag or the arrow keys drive the rail, and a release throws it on momentum before it snaps the nearest station to center under a stop marker.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/camera-rail`

Props:
- `stations`: RailStation[] — Cars evenly spaced along the rail, left to right.
- `value / defaultStation`: string — Controlled or initial station at the platform.
- `onArrive`: (id) => void — Fires the station id once it settles at the platform.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Rail stage height in px.

Notes:
- The frame is a focusable group — arrows ride one station, Home and End run to the ends; cars ride to center on click.
- A release throws the rail on momentum then snaps to the nearest station, deduped against the last arrival.
- Under reduced motion every gesture jumps to its nearest station with no glide or momentum.

### Pivot Grid (KQ-089) — `pivot-grid`
Click a card; the wall turns into its room.

A wall of cards that turns a corner — clicking one pivots the whole assembly a quarter turn on its hinge so the wall swings away edge-on and that card's room swings in to face you; Escape or the room's back control turns you back to the wall.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pivot-grid`

Props:
- `cards`: PivotCard[] — Wall cards; each carries an optional room face.
- `columns`: number (default 3) — Wall grid columns.
- `onEnter`: (id | null) => void — Fires the entered card id, null on return to the wall.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Cards are real buttons — Enter turns into the room, Escape or the back control turns home; focus follows into and out of the room.
- onEnter fires the card id on entry and null on return, deduped.
- Under reduced motion the pivot is an instant swap between the wall grid and the room.

### Elevator Nav (KQ-090) — `elevator-nav`
Call a floor; the doors part on arrival.

Section navigation as an elevator ride — calling a floor rides the car past the floors between, a dial ticking through the numbers as they pass, and two doors part on arrival to reveal the floor; a new call shuts them and rides on.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/elevator-nav`

Props:
- `floors`: Floor[] — Floors top to bottom; the call panel mirrors the order.
- `value / defaultValue`: string — Controlled or initial floor.
- `onArrive`: (id) => void — Fires the floor id once the doors part on arrival.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Shaft stage height in px.

Notes:
- Call-panel buttons and the shaft take arrow keys to change floors; the dial derives its number from the car, never a per-frame write.
- A new call shuts the doors, rides, and parts them on the settle; onArrive fires once per arrival, deduped.
- Under reduced motion the floor swaps instantly, the dial jumps, and the doors skip their travel.

### Glass Pane (KQ-091) — `glass-pane`
Sheets that part for your hand.

Stacked translucent sheets that separate in depth when your hand arrives — each glass slides out along its own diagonal on the glide spring while a specular highlight tracks the pointer across the top sheet.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/glass-pane`

Props:
- `sheets`: { id, label, content }[] — Two to four sheets, rear first.
- `separation`: number (default 12) — Peak separation per sheet step, in px.
- `height`: number (default 230) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Keyboard focus inside any sheet separates the stack exactly like hover.
- Under reduced motion the sheets rest slightly fanned with a fixed highlight.

### Foil Card (KQ-092) — `foil-card`
The light moves, not the card.

A holographic foil card where only the light moves — the sheen chases the pointer across fine striping, rotating hue with the travel, and pressing stamps a bright flash into the foil.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/foil-card`

Props:
- `children / emblem`: ReactNode / ReactNode — Card content, and foil-only artwork for the strongest sheen.
- `intensity`: number (default 0.6) — Sheen strength, 0-1.
- `onStamp`: () => void — Fires on the press flash.

Notes:
- The whole card is a real button — Space and Enter stamp it, and the focus ring is never removed.
- Under reduced motion the sheen rests at a fixed angle and the stamp is an opacity-only flash.

### Slat Wall (KQ-093) — `slat-wall`
Seven louvers, one swap.

A louver wall — content sliced across horizontal slats that each rotate on their own axis in a cascading wave to swap between two faces; every slat is a window onto the full face, so any content survives the slicing.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/slat-wall`

Props:
- `a / b`: ReactNode / ReactNode — The wall's two full-size faces.
- `side / defaultSide / onSideChange`: "a" | "b" / same / (side) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled facing side.
- `slats / height`: number / number (default 7 / 220) — Louver count and wall height.
- `aria-label`: string — Required — the wall is one switch button.

Notes:
- One real role=switch button — Space and Enter flip the wall and the facing side is announced.
- Under reduced motion the faces crossfade whole with no wave.

### Fold Out (KQ-094) — `fold-out`
Creases that open in order.

An origami strip unfolding in staged hinges — one progress spring drives every crease, each panel's hinge mapped to a later slice of the travel so the sheet opens stage by stage with shading lifting as each fold flattens.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/fold-out`

Props:
- `panels`: ReactNode[] — Up to four panels, unfolding top to bottom.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled fold state.
- `panelHeight`: number (default 88) — Height of each panel in px.
- `openLabel / closeLabel`: string / string — Toggle wording.

Notes:
- The toggle is a real button with aria-expanded; the strip height animates with the folds so layout follows honestly.
- Under reduced motion panels simply appear and disappear with a fast fade.

### Height Field (KQ-095) — `height-field`
Sculpt the ground; the lines follow.

A canvas terrain you sculpt by hand — a gaussian brush raises or carves the ground while survey isolines redraw as the field settles back toward its seeded base; the loop idles the moment nothing moves.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/height-field`

Props:
- `levels / brush / relax`: number / number / number (default 5 / 42 / 0.985) — Isoline count, brush radius in px, and settle retention.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- RAISE and CARVE are real chip buttons; the canvas is presentational behind a labelled wrapper with a described interaction.
- The loop pauses offscreen and in hidden tabs, idles when settled, and under reduced motion renders one static seeded frame.

### Curtain Lift (KQ-096) — `curtain-lift`
A hem that travels as a wave.

A draped curtain of sine-fold strips over a stage — raising it sends the folds up with a center-out stagger so the hem travels as a wave, or part mode compresses the halves toward the wings.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/curtain-lift`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The stage the curtain conceals.
- `mode`: "lift" | "part" (default "lift") — Straight up, or split to the wings.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled rig state.
- `folds / height`: number / number (default 14 / 220) — Strip count and stage height.

Notes:
- One real toggle button with aria-expanded runs the rig, and the curtain state is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the curtain fades in place with no wave.

### Mirror Hall (KQ-097) — `mirror-hall`
Reflections that bend to your hand.

A hall of mirrors — the plate recedes through scaled, dimmed, alternately-flipped copies toward a vanishing point, and the corridor bends toward the pointer with the deepest reflections swinging farthest.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/mirror-hall`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The reflected plate — keep it light, it renders N times.
- `copies / falloff / bend`: number / number / number (default 5 / 0.86 / 14) — Reflection count, scale step, and corridor bend per depth.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Only the front plate is real — every reflection is aria-hidden and inert.
- Under reduced motion the corridor rests centered with no bend.

### Frost Wipe (KQ-098) — `frost-wipe`
Wipe to read; stillness frosts it back.

A frosted pane over live content — wipe it clear with the pointer and the frost regrows after a moment of stillness; the grain is seeded per instance and the regrow loop runs only while there is frost to grow.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/frost-wipe`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The content under the glass — always real DOM for AT.
- `brush / refrostDelay`: number / number (default 26 / 1600) — Wipe radius in px and the idle delay before regrowth.
- `height`: number (default 220) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- WIPE CLEAR and REFROST chips give the keyboard the same control, and the pane state is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the refrost is instant rather than gradual; the regrow loop pauses offscreen and in hidden tabs.

### Crumple Sheet (KQ-099) — `crumple-sheet`
Wad it up; smooth it out.

Dismiss crumples the sheet into a paper ball — a deterministic facet mesh converges over the fading content, shading by how far each triangle collapsed, and the wad sits there as a button until you smooth it back out with a settle wobble.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/crumple-sheet`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The sheet's content.
- `crumpled / defaultCrumpled / onCrumpleChange`: boolean / boolean / (crumpled) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled wad state.
- `dismissLabel / restoreLabel`: string / string — Chip and wad wording.
- `height`: number (default 220) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The wad is a real button — focus hops from the dismiss chip to the wad and back, and both states are announced.
- Under reduced motion the mesh never mounts; the sheet and wad swap with a fast fade.

### Lenticular Card (KQ-100) — `lenticular-card`
Two faces, one ridge at a time.

A ridged-lens card holding two faces — sweeping across it flips one vertical ridge at a time, the wave trailing your hand, with a sheen band riding the active ridge; a house slider gives the keyboard the same sweep.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/lenticular-card`

Props:
- `a / b`: ReactNode / ReactNode — The two full-size faces, sliced across the ridges.
- `ridges`: number (default 14) — Strip count, clamped 4-28.
- `onDominantChange`: (side: "a" | "b") => void — Fires once when the majority face crosses over.
- `height`: number (default 210) — Card height in px.

Notes:
- The slider is always rendered and is the accessible control — arrows sweep by five, Home and End jump, and pointer sweeps keep its value honest.
- Under reduced motion pointer tracking is off and the slider sweeps with instant flips.

### Vanish Type (KQ-101) — `vanish-type`
One word in the slot at a time.

A statement set along a ray to its vanishing point — scroll pulls each word into the reading slot in turn while the rest recede up the ray or drift off behind, every position scrubbed 1:1 from scroll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/vanish-type`

Props:
- `words`: string[] — Four to ten words of one statement.
- `onWordChange`: (index) => void — Fires as the reading slot changes words.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- The full statement lives in an sr-only paragraph — the visual words are presentational, and the region scrolls natively from the keyboard.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a plain wrapped headline with the active word underlined.

### Extrude Title (KQ-102) — `extrude-title`
Type with a block behind it.

A headline extruded in layered shadow — the block tilts with the pointer while the extrusion counter-rotates so the light reads as fixed, and pressing crushes the depth flat before it pops back on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/extrude-title`

Props:
- `children`: string — The title text — a string, it is layered.
- `depth / stepPx`: number / number (default 8 / 1.1) — Extrusion steps and offset per step.
- `onPress`: () => void — Fires on the flattening press.

Notes:
- The title is a real button — Space and Enter flatten it exactly like a pointer press.
- Under reduced motion the extrusion is static and presses flatten with fast tweens instead of springs.

### Orbit Tags (KQ-103) — `orbit-tags`
A sphere of chips, one in reach.

A selectable tag sphere — chips ride a golden-angle lattice you can spin with momentum, arrows walk the roving tab stop while the sphere rotates each tag to the front, and selection glides its chip to face you.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/orbit-tags`

Props:
- `tags`: { id, label }[] — Up to sixteen chips on the lattice.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled single selection.
- `radius / height`: number / number (default 92 / 240) — Sphere radius and stage height.

Notes:
- Every chip is a real button on a roving tab stop; the ambient spin pauses under keyboard focus, hover, and offscreen.
- Under reduced motion the sphere renders as a flat chip cloud with the same selection and keyboard.

### Path Type (KQ-104) — `path-type`
Type that rides its own wave.

A sentence set on an undulating ribbon — scroll draws the path in while the type rides its wave, cresting and dipping with a leading tick, every position scrubbed 1:1 from scroll.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/path-type`

Props:
- `text`: string — One sentence to set on the ribbon.
- `amplitude / height`: number / number (default 26 / 260) — Wave height and scroll stage height.
- `onProgress`: (progress) => void — Streams the ride, deduped to hundredths.

Notes:
- The sentence lives in an sr-only paragraph; the SVG is presentational and the region scrolls natively from the keyboard.
- Under reduced motion the ribbon renders fully drawn with the type at rest.

### Billboard Run (KQ-105) — `billboard-run`
Copy you drive past.

Headlines on angled roadside billboards — scroll drives past them down a converging road, each board growing from the vanishing point, banking by the camera, and sweeping off the frame, all scrubbed 1:1.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/billboard-run`

Props:
- `boards`: { id, headline, deck? }[] — Three to six billboards along the road.
- `onPass`: (index) => void — Fires as each board sweeps past the camera.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only ordered list carries every headline; the road and boards are presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the boards render as a flat stacked list with the nearest one accented.

### Turn Word (KQ-106) — `turn-word`
Only the letters that differ turn.

One word becomes another letter by letter — cells half-turn edge-on, swap their glyph, and land on the snap spring in a cascade, while letters the two words share hold perfectly still.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/turn-word`

Props:
- `words`: [string, string] — The two states; the shorter is padded with figure spaces.
- `active / defaultActive / onTurn`: 0 | 1 / 0 | 1 / (active) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled facing word.
- `interactive`: boolean (default true) — Whether the word itself is a switch button.

Notes:
- Interactive mode is a real role=switch — Space and Enter turn it, and the settled word is announced.
- Under reduced motion the words crossfade whole with no letter turns.

### Converge Quote (KQ-107) — `converge-quote`
Lines that land in their own time.

A pull-quote scattered through depth — each line floats at its own distance and jitter until scroll assembles them onto one readable plane in staggered bands, the attribution arriving last under its rule.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/converge-quote`

Props:
- `lines`: string[] — Two to five lines of one quote.
- `attribution`: string — Arrives after the last line lands.
- `onConverge`: (converged) => void — Fires crossing 85% assembly, both directions.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only blockquote carries the full quote; the floating lines are presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the quote renders assembled and static.

### Shadow Script (KQ-108) — `shadow-script`
The shadow has something to say.

Drag the lamp along its arc and the type's cast shadow stretches, skews, and finally becomes legible — because the shadow is a second message, sharpening as the light swings low.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/shadow-script`

Props:
- `text / secret`: string / string — The standing headline and the message hidden in its shadow.
- `defaultAngle`: number (default 0) — Light angle, -60 to 60 degrees.
- `onReveal`: (revealed) => void — Fires crossing legibility in either direction.

Notes:
- The lamp is a real slider — arrows nudge by four degrees, Home and End reach the extremes, and aria-valuetext reads the angle.
- Both texts always live in an sr-only line; under reduced motion the shadow renders pre-stretched and legible.

### Punch Type (KQ-109) — `punch-type`
Letters as windows.

A word die-cut through the surface — the letterforms are true SVG mask cutouts onto a vista behind, and sweeping the pointer shifts the scene so the windows fill with moving color.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/punch-type`

Props:
- `text`: string — One short word, punched at display size.
- `scene`: ReactNode — The layer behind; defaults to a deterministic vista.
- `depth / height`: number / number (default 16 / 200) — Max scene counter-shift and stage height.

Notes:
- The plate is a labelled image with an adjacent interaction note; nothing inside is focusable.
- Under reduced motion and coarse pointers the vista rests at a fixed offset.

### Helix Index (KQ-110) — `helix-index`
Wind the spiral; read the front.

An index wound on a spiral staircase — winding the coil also travels it, so the front record always reads mid-stage; drag, scroll a notch, or arrow through, and Enter pulls the record facing you.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/helix-index`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, hint? }[] — Five to twelve records on the coil.
- `value / defaultValue / onSelect`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled selection; default also seats the coil.
- `radius / pitch / stepDeg / height`: number / number / number / number (default 96 / 34 / 40 / 260) — Coil geometry and stage height.

Notes:
- One roving tab stop — arrows wind one record, Home and End glide the coil, and the settled record is announced.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a flat list with the same buttons and selection.

### Ring Dial (KQ-111) — `ring-dial`
Stops that pull like magnets.

A lens ring in perspective — a knurled band whose detents pull the value in like magnets as you cross them; the fixed needle blinks on every land and the hub pulses the committed stop.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ring-dial`

Props:
- `min / max / detents / step`: number / number / number[] / number (default 0 / 100 / — / 10) — Range and stops; explicit detents win over the step grid.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange / onInput`: number / number / (value) => void / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled value; change fires per land, input streams live.
- `format`: (value) => string — Formats the hub readout, band numerals, and aria-valuetext.
- `size`: number (default 220) — Dial diameter in px.

Notes:
- A real slider — arrows step between detents, Home and End reach the first and last, and aria-valuetext speaks the formatted stop.
- Under reduced motion the ring renders flat and tracks the pointer one-to-one with no magnetic pull; the needle blink remains as the land cue.

### Z Accordion (KQ-112) — `z-accordion`
Sections that step back, not down.

An accordion that spends depth instead of height — the open sheet glides to the front while the rest step back into the rack, scaled, dimmed, and stacked so every header stays in reach.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/z-accordion`

Props:
- `items`: { id, title, content }[] — The sheets; three to five read best.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open sheet — exactly one at a time.

Notes:
- True accordion semantics — header buttons carry aria-expanded, panels are labelled regions, and arrows, Home, and End move between headers.
- Under reduced motion it renders as a flat accordion in normal flow with instant expand and no recession.

### Depth Menu (KQ-113) — `depth-menu`
Branches recede; the trail hangs behind.

An inline menu that spends depth on hierarchy — entering a branch pushes the current level back into a hanging trail, a breadcrumb rail floats above for multi-level jumps, and focus always returns to the row you came from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/depth-menu`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, hint?, children? }[] — The tree; branch nodes carry children.
- `onSelect`: (id, path) => void — Fires on a leaf with its ancestor path.
- `depthGap`: number (default 80) — Perspective-honest recession per level.

Notes:
- WAI-ARIA menu keyboard — arrows move, Right or Enter descends, Left or Escape pops with focus returned, and level changes are announced.
- Under reduced motion levels swap instantly and the breadcrumb alone carries the context.

### Stage Tabs (KQ-114) — `stage-tabs`
Panels on a revolve.

Tabs staged like a theater revolve — the outgoing panel sweeps to one wing as the incoming one revolves in from the other, direction following tab order, over a floor shadow that squashes with the turn.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/stage-tabs`

Props:
- `tabs`: { id, label, content }[] — The tab set.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active tab.
- `minHeight`: number (default 180) — Stage floor height in px.

Notes:
- Real tabs — roving tabindex, arrows auto-activate with wrap, Home and End jump, and the panel itself is focusable.
- Under reduced motion panels crossfade instantly and the underline jumps without gliding.

### Card Fan (KQ-115) — `card-fan`
Fan, sweep, play.

A hand of cards that fans open around a shared pivot — sweep to raise one, siblings part around it, and picking flies the card to a played slot where it lands on the recoil spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/card-fan`

Props:
- `cards`: { id, label, content }[] — Up to seven cards in the hand.
- `value / defaultValue / onSelect`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled played card.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled fan state.
- `cardWidth`: number (default 120) — Card width in px; height is 1.45×.

Notes:
- The open hand is a radiogroup — arrows move the raised card, Enter or Space plays it, Escape folds the hand and returns focus to the stack.
- Under reduced motion the fan opens flat and instantly, raising is a highlight, and picks move without flight or bump.

### Hallway Menu (KQ-116) — `hallway-menu`
Every stop looks down its own hallway.

A mega-menu of parallel hallways — sweeping the rail looks down each corridor in one-point perspective, doors hinge off the walls toward a far-wall plate, and the whole view swings sideways between stops.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hallway-menu`

Props:
- `hallways`: { id, label, items: { id, label, hint? }[] }[] — Two to five hallways on the rail.
- `onSelect`: (hallwayId, itemId) => void — Fires when a door is chosen.
- `corridorHeight`: number (default 220) — Corridor stage height in px.

Notes:
- Rail buttons carry aria-expanded; ArrowDown enters the corridor, arrows walk the doors in documented wall order, and Escape returns to the rail.
- Under reduced motion each corridor renders as a flat two-column panel with instant open and the same keyboard.

### Deck Switcher (KQ-117) — `deck-switcher`
Views raked back, one zooms forward.

An app-switcher for views — open the deck and every view rakes back into a tilted stack; slide the rake by drag, wheel, or arrows, then commit and the chosen view zooms forward while the rest fold away.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/deck-switcher`

Props:
- `views`: { id, title, content }[] — Two to six views; all stay mounted so their state survives switches.
- `activeId / defaultActiveId / onActiveChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled active view; fires only when the commit changes it.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The open deck is a listbox — arrows slide the rake, Home and End jump, Enter commits, and Escape cancels back to the previous view with focus returned.
- Under reduced motion the deck is a flat list of title cards and commits swap instantly.

### Flyover Map (KQ-118) — `flyover-map`
The page, miniature and tilted.

A scroll region with its own tilted minimap — districts sized to their sections, a live viewport indicator riding the scroll, and a click that flies the view to any district on the glide spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/flyover-map`

Props:
- `sections`: { id, label, content }[] — Three to six sections stacked in the scroll region.
- `onArrive`: (id) => void — Fires when a flight lands or scrolling settles in a new section.
- `height`: number (default 320) — Viewport height in px.

Notes:
- The minimap is a navigation landmark of real buttons — aria-current tracks the active district and arrivals are announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the map renders flat and district clicks jump instantly with no flight.

### Gate Stepper (KQ-119) — `gate-stepper`
The gates ahead stay in sight.

A wizard where progress is distance — every remaining gate is visible ahead, receding through the current one; advancing sweeps the active gate past the camera while the line moves up a slot, and a refused gate shakes its head.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gate-stepper`

Props:
- `steps`: { id, title, content }[] — Two to five gates.
- `index / defaultIndex / onIndexChange`: number / number / (index) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled position.
- `canAdvance`: (index) => boolean — Gate check before Next; a refusal shakes and announces.
- `nextLabel / backLabel / doneLabel / onComplete`: string / string / string / () => void — Footer wording and the finish callback.

Notes:
- The active gate is a labelled group and every change or refusal is announced politely; ahead and passed gates are hidden from AT.
- Under reduced motion it is a flat stepper — no recession, instant swaps, refusals announce without the shake.

### Hinge Nav (KQ-120) — `hinge-nav`
Doors on the frame, not drawers in it.

Navigation doors hinged on the frame edges — pull a side tab and the panel swings in from edge-on over the dimmed stage, bumping the frame as it lands; Escape or the stage swings it shut.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hinge-nav`

Props:
- `doors`: { side: "left" | "right", label, items }[] — One door per side; one open at a time.
- `children`: ReactNode — The stage content the doors swing over.
- `onSelect`: (side, id) => void — Fires when a door row is chosen; the door closes after.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Tabs carry aria-expanded, the door is a labelled region, focus moves to the first row on open and returns to the tab on Escape.
- Under reduced motion doors fade-slide in flat with no rotation or frame bump.

### Pop Book (KQ-121) — `pop-book`
Cutouts that stand up from the fold.

A pop-up spread that opens with scroll — the two page halves relax apart while hinged cutouts stand up from the center fold rank by rank, back row first, every rise scrubbed 1:1.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pop-book`

Props:
- `pieces`: { id, node, row? }[] — Three to six cutouts; row 0 is the back rank.
- `onOpenChange`: (openness) => void — Streams the spread, deduped to twentieths.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only list carries every cutout; the spread is presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the plate renders fully open and static.

### Iso Blocks (KQ-122) — `iso-blocks`
A district that rises to meet you.

An isometric block city drawn entirely in flat 2D — every cuboid is three clip-path faces shaded from one hue, so the projection is Safari-proof by construction; blocks rise to the hand and light their roofs.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iso-blocks`

Props:
- `blocks`: { id, label, storeys?, hue? }[] — Four to twelve blocks on a near-square grid.
- `onInspect`: (id) => void — Fires when a block is pressed.
- `cell`: number (default 64) — Grid cell width in px.

Notes:
- Every block is a real button — keyboard focus raises and lights it exactly like hover.
- Under reduced motion the city reads as a flat top-down survey with the same buttons.

### Lift Tray (KQ-123) — `lift-tray`
Every tool floats to your hand.

Tools that float up to your hand — each plate levitates off the tray with a contact shadow that shrinks and softens as it rises, neighbors lifting in sympathy, item shadow and ground ellipse two views of one altitude.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/lift-tray`

Props:
- `items`: { id, label, node }[] — Three to five tools on the tray.
- `onPick`: (id) => void — Fires when a tool is pressed.
- `maxLift`: number (default 14) — Peak levitation in px.

Notes:
- Every tool is a real button — keyboard focus levitates it exactly like hover, and picks are announced.
- Under reduced motion hover is a flat highlight and the ground shadows rest static.

### Shaker Dome (KQ-124) — `shaker-dome`
The flakes take the long way down.

A keepsake dome over a diorama — shake it by hand and the seeded flakes swirl off the true semicircular shell, settling on gravity until the scene stands still and the loop stands down.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/shaker-dome`

Props:
- `scene`: ReactNode — The diorama under the glass; defaults to a minted monument.
- `flakes`: number (default 42) — Particle count, capped at eighty.
- `label / height`: ReactNode / number (default nameplate / 240) — Plinth engraving and stage height.

Notes:
- The STIR button gives the keyboard a standard kick and stirs are announced; the sim idles the moment the flakes rest.
- The loop pauses offscreen and in hidden tabs; under reduced motion one settled frame renders and the shake is disabled.

### Daylight Dial (KQ-125) — `daylight-dial`
Drag the sky; the shadows obey.

A time-of-day dial over a skyline — one hour value drives the sky's keyframed color ramp, the sun and moon in anti-phase on their arc, sweeping building shadows with honest projection, and stars that keep their hours.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/daylight-dial`

Props:
- `hour / defaultHour / onHourChange`: number / number / (hour) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled 0-24 clock; settles announce integers.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Scene height in px.

Notes:
- The track is one slider — arrows step an hour, Page keys three, Home and End bound the day, and aria-valuetext reads the clock.
- Naturally reduced-motion-safe: the scene scrubs one-to-one with the dial and merely loses its spring lag.

### Cutout Town (KQ-126) — `cutout-town`
Buildings that stand up from the plan.

A town plan whose buildings stand up from their plots as you scroll — each plot wakes before its band, the facade rotates upright from flat, and a base shadow widens beneath it; windows are lit deterministically per building.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/cutout-town`

Props:
- `buildings`: { id, label, width, height, x }[] — Three to six facades and their plots.
- `onRaised`: (count) => void — How many stand fully, deduped.
- `stageHeight`: number (default 280) — Scroll stage height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only list carries every building; the plan is presentational and the region scrolls natively.
- Under reduced motion the town renders fully built and static.

### Transit Window (KQ-127) — `transit-window`
The near fence outruns the far hills.

A train window whose landscape passes with your scroll — three looping parallax bands at honest speeds, mile markers that file past on the first lap, and a cabin sway that leans only with scroll velocity.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/transit-window`

Props:
- `journey`: number (default 4) — Track length as a multiple of the stage height.
- `onMilestone`: (marker) => void — Fires as each of four mile markers passes.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Cabin height in px.

Notes:
- An sr-only line describes the scene; the region scrolls natively and the distance readout renders without re-renders.
- Under reduced motion the landscape holds a mid-journey frame while the readout still tracks.

### Spotlight Stage (KQ-128) — `spotlight-stage`
Acts step into the light.

A dark stage where one sprung beam is the single truth — the rig cone and floor pool derive from it, every act brightens on a gaussian falloff as the light passes, and a click holds an act in the spot until Escape reopens the stage.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spotlight-stage`

Props:
- `acts`: { id, label, node }[] — Two to four plates on the stage.
- `value / defaultValue / onSpot`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled spotlit act.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Every act is a real button — keyboard focus summons the beam, Enter commits regardless of the spring, Escape reopens the stage.
- Under reduced motion the house lights are even and selection is an accent border with no beam.

### Pull Shelf (KQ-129) — `pull-shelf`
Tip a spine; read the spread.

Books on an instrument ledge — a spine tips out of the rank as your hand arrives, neighbors parting around it, and pulling it flies the book forward to open into a two-page spread while a dashed gap holds its slot.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pull-shelf`

Props:
- `books`: { id, title, spineWidth?, height?, hue?, spread }[] — Four to eight volumes; geometry seeds deterministically per id.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: string | null / same / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open volume — one at a time.

Notes:
- Every spine is a real disclosure button; focus moves to the spread's close chip on open and returns on Escape or close.
- Under reduced motion spreads fade in place and spines highlight flat.

### Turn Model (KQ-130) — `turn-model`
Drawn live, not filmed.

An object turntable drawn live — one yaw value projects the wireframe every frame, splitting edges into dim back and inked front paths, with platter ticks riding the same matrix; drag with momentum into 15° detents.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/turn-model`

Props:
- `model`: { vertices, edges } — A 3D wireframe; defaults to the minted monument.
- `angle / defaultAngle / onAngleChange`: number / number / (angle) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled yaw; settles announce snapped degrees.
- `size / elevationDeg`: number / number (default 220 / 18) — Stage size and the fixed camera tilt.

Notes:
- The stage is one slider — arrows step fifteen degrees, Page keys ninety, Home returns by the shortest path, and aria-valuetext reads the yaw.
- Under reduced motion the platter jumps between detents while the projection still scrubs live.

### Star Warp (KQ-131) — `star-warp`
Hold to jump to warp; steer the streaks.

A starfield streaming past the cabin — hold to jump to warp and the stars stretch into streaks, move to steer the vanishing point toward the pointer, then release and they ease back down to a cruise.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/star-warp`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 180) — Star count, clamped to a sane maximum.
- `onWarp`: (warping) => void — Fires as the drive enters and leaves warp.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Field height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a keyboard Hold-to-warp button, so the effect is fully operable without a pointer.
- The field pauses off-screen and when the tab is hidden, rebasing its clock so the stream never jumps.
- Under reduced motion it paints one static sparse frame with no animation loop.

### Depth Fog (KQ-132) — `depth-fog`
Sweep the fog aside to read beneath it.

Drifting banks of fog roll across the panel at parallax depths and part around the pointer to reveal the survey beneath, closing back in the moment you move on.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/depth-fog`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The content revealed behind the fog (real DOM).
- `onClear`: (clearing) => void — Fires as the pointer starts and stops parting the fog.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Panel height in px.

Notes:
- The content behind is real DOM, so it stays readable to assistive tech regardless of the fog above it.
- The fog pauses off-screen and when hidden, rebasing its drift clock so it never jumps on resume.
- Under reduced motion it holds a static, mostly-parted frame so the content is never trapped behind haze.

### Ember Column (KQ-133) — `ember-column`
The embers lean toward you as you pass.

A column of embers rises off the forge flue, each spark brightening then fading as it climbs and swaying on its own draft; sweep the pointer across and the whole stream leans toward you, easing back to vertical as you leave.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/ember-column`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 90) — Ember count, capped for cheapness.
- `onEmberPass`: (rate) => void — Streams a coarse embers-per-second rate, deduped.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Flue height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a polite live region; the pointer bends the stream with a vertical falloff.
- The column pauses off-screen and when hidden, rebasing its clock so the rise never jumps.
- Under reduced motion it paints one static frozen frame with no animation loop.

### Constellation Map (KQ-134) — `constellation-map`
Turn the sky; links form, click to pin a star.

A cloud of stars you turn with a drag — spin the sky and it coasts to a stop, links stitch between the stars nearest the cursor, and clicking a named star pins it with a label; the named stars are reachable from a keyboard list too.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/constellation-map`

Props:
- `stars`: Star[] — Named, pinnable stars plotted in the cloud.
- `extra`: number (default 60) — Unlabelled filler stars for density.
- `onPin`: (id | null) => void — Fires the pinned star id, null when unpinned.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Sky height in px.

Notes:
- The named stars are reachable from a keyboard button list that pins and orbits one to the front.
- The cloud coasts on release then idle-stops the loop once nothing is moving, to save battery.
- Under reduced motion the sky is static, links forming on hover with a single repaint and no loop.

### Rain Pane (KQ-135) — `rain-pane`
Tap the glass; the rain splashes where you touch.

Rain runs down a glass pane at two depths — near streaks fall fast and bright, far ones slow and faint — and tapping the glass splashes a ring of droplets wherever you touch.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rain-pane`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 70) — Total streaks across both depths, capped.
- `onSplash`: (at) => void — Fires the { x, y } of each tap splash.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Pane height in px.

Notes:
- Cheap and gated — the rain pauses off-screen and when the tab is hidden, rebasing its clock.
- Splashes pool to a fixed cap, so rapid taps stay light; each tap fires onSplash with its point.
- Under reduced motion a few streaks freeze in place and a tap draws one static splash ring.

### Sun Shaft (KQ-136) — `sun-shaft`
Move through the beam; the motes swirl in your wake.

A shaft of light crosses the atrium with dust motes drifting through it, brighter where they cross the beam; move the pointer through and the motes swirl into an eddy in your wake, settling back as you pass.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/sun-shaft`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 70) — Dust mote count, capped for cheapness.
- `onStir`: (stirring) => void — Fires as the pointer enters and leaves the beam.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Panel height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a polite live region; the pointer spins a vortex through nearby motes.
- The shaft pauses off-screen and when hidden, rebasing its clock so the drift never jumps.
- Under reduced motion it paints one static lit frame with the motes at rest and no loop.

### Firefly Field (KQ-137) — `firefly-field`
Hold, and the fireflies gather to your hand.

Fireflies wander and blink at their own depths across a dusk meadow; press and hold and they gather to your hand, milling around it, then disperse back into the dark when you let go.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/firefly-field`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 48) — Firefly count, clamped to a cheap range.
- `onGather`: (gathering) => void — Fires as the hold starts and stops.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Meadow height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a keyboard Gather button (Space or Enter held), so it works without a pointer.
- The field pauses off-screen and when hidden, rebasing its clock over the pause.
- Under reduced motion it holds a calm static scatter with no wander or gather loop.

### Vapor Ring (KQ-138) — `vapor-ring`
Click the air; a ring of vapor rolls out.

Click the still air and a ring of vapor puffs out, its turbulent edge expanding toward you and drifting up as it thins to nothing; the loop sleeps whenever the last ring has faded.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/vapor-ring`

Props:
- `onPuff`: (at) => void — Fires the { x, y } of each puff.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Surface height in px.

Notes:
- Rings pool to a fixed cap, so rapid clicks stay cheap; each click fires onPuff with its point.
- The loop idle-stops the instant the last ring has faded and wakes on the next puff.
- Under reduced motion a click draws one static ring, replaced on the next click, with no loop.

### Gravity Well (KQ-139) — `gravity-well`
Draw the well; fling fast to slingshot bodies free.

Bodies orbit a gravity well that follows the pointer — a softened pull holds them in ellipses, motion trails streaking their paths; flick the well fast and a stream slingshots free before the field draws it back.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gravity-well`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 120) — Orbiting body count, capped for cheapness.
- `onFling`: (flung) => void — Fires when a fling slings bodies free and when it settles.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Field height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a polite live region; the pointer is the well, easing back to center when it leaves.
- The field pauses off-screen and when hidden, rebasing its clock so the orbits never jump.
- Under reduced motion it paints one static frame of concentric orbital rings with no loop.

### Paper Flight (KQ-140) — `paper-flight`
Click the sky; a glider swoops across on its path.

Folded gliders drift across the hangar at their own depths; click the sky and one launches on a bezier flight, banking to face its path and scaling with the arc before it fades at the far edge.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/paper-flight`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 7) — Ambient drifting gliders, clamped to a cheap range.
- `onLaunch`: (index) => void — Fires the launched flight index per launch.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Hangar height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a keyboard Launch button, so a flight can be sent without a pointer.
- Flights pool to a fixed cap, so rapid clicks stay cheap; each launch fires onLaunch with its index.
- Under reduced motion the gliders pose at rest and a launch drops one static mid-flight glider, no loop.

### Terrain Relief (KQ-141) — `terrain-relief`
Orbit the relief; a node reads its elevation.

A data heightmap drawn as a rotatable wireframe terrain — the grid projects into an oblique surface you orbit by dragging, strands painter-ordered by depth and fading into the distance, and hovering a node lifts a readout of its elevation.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/terrain-relief`

Props:
- `data`: number[][] — Elevations by [row][col]; normalized internally.
- `onProbe`: (probe | null) => void — Fires the probed { row, col, value }, null on leave.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Chart height in px.

Notes:
- Drag orbits the surface; ArrowLeft/Right rotate and Home resets to the default oblique angle.
- A node hover or focus fires onProbe with the elevation in original units and lifts a readout.
- Under reduced motion the surface holds one static oblique frame while probing stays live.

### Little Planet (KQ-142) — `little-planet`
Spin the planet; a section curves up to the top.

Sections wrapped around a tiny planet's rim — spin the planet and each curves up to the top slot, growing to full size as it surfaces while the far side tucks behind the horizon; release snaps the nearest section home.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/little-planet`

Props:
- `sections`: PlanetSection[] — Sections spaced evenly around the planet rim.
- `value / defaultValue`: string — Controlled or initial surfaced section id.
- `onSurface`: (id) => void — Fires the section id once it settles at the top.
- `radius / height`: number (default 96 / 320) — Planet radius and stage height in px.

Notes:
- The planet is a focusable control — arrows spin one section to the top, Home returns to the first.
- A release snaps the nearest section to the top the short way; the controlled value is honored.
- Under reduced motion the sections sit in a static ring and surface with an instant jump.

### Arc Routes (KQ-143) — `arc-routes`
Hover a route; a rider arcs to its port.

Great-circle arcs lifting off a flat map — pins plot ports across the panel and each route bows a quadratic arc between two of them, arching higher the longer the hop; hovering or focusing a route brightens its arc and rides a marker along it from origin to destination.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/arc-routes`

Props:
- `pins`: RoutePin[] — Ports plotted by normalized x, y across the map.
- `routes`: Route[] — Arcs between pins, referenced by from and to ids.
- `onRide`: (routeId | null) => void — Fires the ridden route id, null when it ends.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Map height in px.

Notes:
- Routes ride from a legend of real buttons, so they are keyboard-reachable and announce on focus.
- The rider is a bezier-derived marker travelling a tween from origin to destination while the route is active.
- Under reduced motion no rider travels; the arc brightens and marks its midpoint instead.

### Radar Scope (KQ-144) — `radar-scope`
The sweep lights each contact; click to inspect.

A polar approach scope whose sweep beam rakes the display once a cycle, lighting each contact as it crosses its bearing and leaving it to fade in afterglow; click a contact to pin a callout, or reach every one through the keyboard list beside the scope.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/radar-scope`

Props:
- `blips`: Blip[] — Contacts placed by bearing (deg) and range (0..1).
- `onInspect`: (id | null) => void — Fires the inspected contact id, null when cleared.
- `sweepPeriod`: number (default 4) — Seconds per full sweep rotation.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Scope height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is aria-hidden with a parallel button list, so every contact is inspectable from the keyboard.
- The sweep pauses off-screen and when the tab is hidden, rebasing its clock so it never jumps on resume.
- Under reduced motion the scope paints one static frame with every contact lit and no animation loop.

### Fisheye Grid (KQ-145) — `fisheye-grid`
Sweep the lens; tiles bulge up beneath it.

A magnifier you sweep across a grid — tiles under the lens bulge up and spread apart in a radial fisheye, the largest riding to the front while the rest ease back to rest, and the tile at the lens center reads out as you go.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/fisheye-grid`

Props:
- `cells`: FisheyeCell[] — Tiles laid on a near-square grid under the lens.
- `onFocusCell`: (id | null) => void — Fires the tile at the lens center, null off-grid.
- `lensRadius / strength`: number (default 110 / 0.6) — Lens reach in px and bulge magnitude.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Grid height in px.

Notes:
- On fine pointers the lens glides under the cursor; on touch it drags a puck; arrows move it a tile at a time.
- Every tile derives its bulge from the shared lens value, so nothing writes state per pointer-move.
- Under reduced motion the lens drops and a discrete focused tile scales up in place instead.

### Section Cut (KQ-146) — `section-cut`
Drag the plane; a stratum opens at the cut.

Drag a cutting plane down through a layered core sample and the block above the plane clips away, opening each stratum at a hatched section face as the plane crosses it, its label called out and a depth readout tracking the cut.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/section-cut`

Props:
- `strata`: Stratum[] — Bands top to bottom, each with a thickness and tone.
- `onCut`: (id | null) => void — Fires the stratum at the cut face, null above grade.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Core height in px.

Notes:
- The plane is a role=slider — drag tracks 1:1, arrows step it, PageUp/Down jump a whole stratum.
- onCut reports the intersected stratum, deduped; the depth readout derives from the plane, never a per-frame write.
- Under reduced motion the plane and the clip resolve instantly with the same section semantics.

### Compass Needle (KQ-147) — `compass-needle`
Drag the dial; the needle wobbles to a point.

A bearing picker with a magnetic settle — the needle tracks the pointer bearing as you drag around the rose, then on release swings the short way to the nearest cardinal detent and wobbles home like a magnetized compass; the arrow keys step it point by point.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/compass-needle`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue`: number — Controlled or initial bearing, 0 (N) clockwise to 359.
- `onChange`: (deg) => void — Fires the settled bearing once it snaps to a detent.
- `detent`: number (default 45) — Detent grid in degrees; 45 gives the 8-point rose.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Dial size in px.

Notes:
- The dial is a role=slider — arrows step one detent, Home returns to north, and the value text names the cardinal point.
- Release snaps the short way to the nearest detent on the recoil spring, its two bounces reading as magnetism.
- Under reduced motion the needle jumps to the pointer bearing and the detent with no wobble.

### Blueprint Rise (KQ-148) — `blueprint-rise`
Scroll the plan up into a standing model.

A flat floor plan that stands up as you scroll — the plan tilts from a top-down view to a three-quarter oblique while every wall extrudes off its floor line into a line-art model, a rise readout tracking the climb from plan to structure.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/blueprint-rise`

Props:
- `walls`: WallSeg[] — Wall segments in 0..1 plan coordinates, with a height.
- `labels`: { id, x, y, text }[] — Room labels on the floor plane.
- `journey / height`: number (default 3 / 300) — Scroll track length in stages and stage height in px.
- `onRise`: (progress) => void — Streams the rise 0..1, deduped to twentieths.

Notes:
- Pure SVG faux-3D — no CSS 3D — so the extruded model stays Safari-proof.
- An sr-only line describes the plan; the region scrolls natively and the model is presentational.
- Under reduced motion the model holds a mid-rise composition while the readout still tracks scroll.

### Explode View (KQ-149) — `explode-view`
Toggle to fan the assembly apart, labelled.

A stacked assembly that separates on command — toggle it and the parts fan apart along an isometric axis in a staggered cascade, a leader line running from each part to its label; toggle back and they nest into one solid again.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/explode-view`

Props:
- `parts`: Part[] — Assembly parts, ordered bottom to top.
- `exploded / defaultExploded`: boolean — Controlled or initial exploded state.
- `onToggle`: (exploded) => void — Fires the new state when the assembly is toggled.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Parts are focusable buttons — hovering or focusing one lights the part and its leader-lined label.
- The separation staggers on a cascade so the outer parts lead; the controlled exploded prop is honored.
- Under reduced motion the assembly swaps instantly between nested and fanned, leaders and all.

### Matrix Rise (KQ-150) — `matrix-rise`
Toggle the grid up into a city of bars.

A heat grid that stands up into a bar city — toggle it and every cell extrudes into an isometric bar whose height tracks its value, the field rising in a diagonal cascade; toggle back and it lies flat as a heatmap again.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/matrix-rise`

Props:
- `data`: number[][] — The value matrix; drives both color and bar height.
- `rowLabels / colLabels`: string[] — Optional axis labels.
- `risen / defaultRisen`: boolean — Controlled or initial raised state.
- `onToggle`: (risen) => void — Fires the new state when the grid is toggled.

Notes:
- The bars are clip-path faces, not CSS 3D, so the city stays Safari-proof.
- Hovering or focusing a cell reads its value; the controlled risen prop is honored.
- Under reduced motion the grid swaps instantly between heatmap and city.

### Swing Door (KQ-151) — `swing-door`
Past the detent or slammed home.

A hinged door with handle physics — drag it past the detent and it swings wide on the glide spring; let go early and it slams home, shuddering the frame and puffing a dust line at the jamb.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/swing-door`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — What waits in the doorway.
- `doorFace`: ReactNode — Artwork on the door; defaults to a minted panel.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled door state.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Frame height in px.

Notes:
- The handle is a real button with aria-expanded; Escape slams the door and focus returns to the handle.
- Under reduced motion the door slides flat with no slam or shudder.

### Hatch Board (KQ-152) — `hatch-board`
Every lid rides its own spring.

A grid of spring-loaded hatches — every lid pops open on its own snap spring to reveal the well beneath, latches shut with a click pulse, and the whole board can cascade open or closed from a corner.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hatch-board`

Props:
- `hatches`: { id, label, lid, well }[] — Four to nine hatches; the label names each disclosure.
- `openIds / defaultOpenIds / onOpenChange`: string[] / string[] / (openIds) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled open set.
- `columns / cellAspect`: number / number (default 3 / 1.1) — Board shape.

Notes:
- Each lid is a real disclosure button; wells are labelled regions, inert while shut, and the open count is announced.
- Under reduced motion lids fade flat with no pops or cascade.

### Gear Train (KQ-153) — `gear-train`
Ratios you can feel.

Three meshed gears with honest ratios — drag any wheel and the transmission carries your hand to a needle on the output shaft; teeth stay interleaved at every angle by construction, and releases coast on a velocity-seeded drift.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gear-train`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled needle value, 0-100; fires once per settle.
- `teeth`: [number, number, number] (default [12, 18, 24]) — Drive, idler, and output tooth counts.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The train is one slider — arrows step by two, Page keys by ten, Home and End glide the rails, and every settle is announced by value.
- Under reduced motion gears track the hand one-to-one with no coast; the tooth-tick blink remains as an opacity cue.

### Commit Lever (KQ-154) — `commit-lever`
Past the guard, then all the way.

A two-stage action lever — the pull stiffens at the guard notch and the flap swings aside as you push through to arm; full travel commits with a thunk, locks, and glides home after the reset.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/commit-lever`

Props:
- `onCommit`: () => void — Required — fires exactly once per full travel.
- `onArmChange`: (armed) => void — Tracks the armed stage, deduped.
- `commitLabel / resetDelay / disabled`: string / number / boolean (default "Commit" / 900 / false) — Wording, auto-return delay (0 holds), and the safety.

Notes:
- The grip is a real button — first press arms, second commits, Escape disarms; every stage is announced.
- Under reduced motion the lever snaps between its three stations with no thunk or lamp blink.

### Crank Reel (KQ-155) — `crank-reel`
Wind forward; the freewheel only clicks.

A hand-crank that winds progress onto a visible spool — forward turns bank units with ratchet ticks while backward cranking just chatters on the freewheel; the drum trails the handle on the glide spring.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/crank-reel`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: number / number / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled wound units; fires per unit.
- `max / unitsPerTurn / reversible`: number / number / boolean (default 12 / 2 / false) — Capacity, gearing, and whether backward unwinds.
- `onSlip / spoolLabel`: () => void / string — Freewheel slip callback and the wound material's name.

Notes:
- The rig is one slider — arrows wind a unit, Home and End run the rails with a single batched announcement, and slips announce as freewheel.
- Under reduced motion the crank and drum move instantly and the pawl kick becomes an opacity blink.

### Pulley Lift (KQ-156) — `pulley-lift`
The counterweight lands the car.

A dumbwaiter on one honest travel value — haul the platform, the counterweight, or either rope fall, and the car settles to the nearest floor with an elastic arrival while the sheave turns by exactly the rope it passed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/pulley-lift`

Props:
- `floors`: { id, label, content }[] — Two to four floors, ground first.
- `floor / defaultFloor / onFloorChange`: string / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled floor; fires on settle.
- `height`: number (default 280) — Shaft height in px.

Notes:
- The frame is a vertical slider and every floor tab is a real button with aria-current; arrivals are announced by label.
- Under reduced motion the car jumps between floors with no glide or bounce.

### Combo Dials (KQ-157) — `combo-dials`
Three dials, one agreement.

A combination lock of stacked cylinder dials — digits sweep the rim under your drag, settle into detents with a click, and when every dial agrees with the setting the bolt slides open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/combo-dials`

Props:
- `length`: number (default 3) — Dial count, two to five.
- `value / defaultValue / onValueChange`: string / string / (value) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled digits; fires per settled digit.
- `secret / onUnlock`: string / () => void — The setting to match and the single-fire unlock callback.

Notes:
- Each dial is a spinbutton — arrows step with wrap, typing a digit glides straight to it, and settles are announced per dial.
- Under reduced motion digits jump detent by detent and the bolt opens with a fast fade; near-misses never announce proximity.

### Zipper Seam (KQ-158) — `zipper-seam`
The seam parts above the pull.

Fabric halves joined by a toothed seam — draw the pull down and they part in a V above it, teeth shearing aside along the edges, with a shiver when the yank lands fully open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/zipper-seam`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — What lies in the seam.
- `progress / defaultProgress / onProgressChange`: number / number / (progress) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled 0-100 opening; fires on settle.
- `height`: number (default 260) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- The pull is a vertical slider — arrows step by five, Page keys by twenty, Home and End seal and open; ends snap and announce.
- Under reduced motion the seam tracks the hand one-to-one with no snaps or shiver.

### Trapdoor Drop (KQ-159) — `trapdoor-drop`
The long way down, on purpose.

A two-stage disposal platform — arming slides the bolts aside while the item wobbles, confirming swings both leaves down from their outer hinges, and the item accelerates into the well past blinking depth rings before the doors clap shut.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/trapdoor-drop`

Props:
- `children / itemKey`: ReactNode / string — The item on the doors; change the key to lower in the next one.
- `onDrop`: () => void — Fires exactly once when the item has fallen through.
- `armLabel / dropLabel / disarmLabel`: string / string / string — The two-stage wording.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- Dismissal is always two presses — arm, then confirm — with Escape backing out; every stage is announced.
- Under reduced motion the item fades instantly on confirm with the same two-stage semantics.

### Drawbridge (KQ-160) — `drawbridge`
Chains out, deck down, crossing proved.

A span between two content islands — lowering pays the chains out from the gantry as the deck swings down, thumps the far abutment on the recoil, and a runner crosses once to prove the connection before the far bank wakes.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/drawbridge`

Props:
- `near / far`: ReactNode / ReactNode — The two banks; the far one is reachable only while bridged.
- `bridged / defaultBridged / onBridgedChange`: boolean / boolean / (bridged) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled span state.
- `lowerLabel / raiseLabel`: string / string — Toggle wording.
- `height`: number (default 250) — Stage height in px.

Notes:
- One real toggle with aria-expanded; the far bank is inert and dimmed until the deck lands, and both states are announced.
- Under reduced motion the deck fades between raised and bridged with no chains or runner.

### Chip Cloud (KQ-161) — `chip-cloud`
Pick a chip; it flies to the tray.

A multi-select field split in two: a tray of what you have picked and a cloud of what is left. Picking a chip flies the very same pill out of the cloud and into the tray while the survivors close the gap; picking it again flies it home.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/chip-cloud`

Props:
- `chips`: Chip[] — The full roster; picked chips gather in the tray.
- `value / defaultValue`: string[] — Controlled or initial picks, in pick order.
- `onValueChange`: (ids) => void — Fires the picked ids whenever the set changes.
- `label / placeholder`: ReactNode / string — Group label, and the copy shown while the tray is empty.

Notes:
- Every chip is a real toggle button, so the field is operable with Tab and Space alone.
- A picked chip changes container, which replaces its DOM node — the chip is re-focused as it lands so the keyboard never loses its place.
- Under reduced motion chips reposition in a single frame; same picks, order, and announcements.

### Rating Arc (KQ-162) — `rating-arc`
Sweep the arc; the score fills to your thumb.

A rating that fills along a 220-degree arc — the fill draws itself and the thumb rides the curve rather than cutting across it, while sweeping the arc paints a ghost of the score you would commit.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/rating-arc`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue`: number — Controlled or initial score, 0..max.
- `onValueChange`: (value) => void — Fires the committed score.
- `max / step`: number (default 5 / 1) — Top of the scale and its granularity; 0.5 gives half marks.
- `label / readout`: string / boolean (default "Rating" / true) — Accessible name, and whether to print the score beneath.

Notes:
- Reports as a slider — arrows step, Home and End jump the ends, and aria-valuetext reads the score rather than a bare number.
- The hover ghost is driven straight off motion values, so sweeping the arc never costs a React render.
- Under reduced motion the fill and thumb land in a single frame; same scale and commits.

### Swatch Lock (KQ-163) — `swatch-lock`
The pick locks; the rest step back.

A grid of colour swatches where the pick locks in: a ring draws itself around the chosen chip while the rest ease back to let it stand alone. Locking is the whole affordance — you can see what is held without reading anything.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/swatch-lock`

Props:
- `swatches`: Swatch[] — Each carries an id, a label, and any CSS colour.
- `value / defaultValue`: string — Controlled or initial pick.
- `name`: string — Renders a hidden native radio per swatch so forms post the pick.
- `columns`: number (default 5) — Grid columns; Up and Down step by exactly this many.

Notes:
- Full APG radio keyboard on a roving tabindex — Left and Right walk the roster and wrap, Up and Down step a row, Home and End jump the ends, Space selects.
- Colour is never the only cue: every swatch carries a label, and the label is what assistive tech announces.
- Under reduced motion the ring is simply there and the dim is a colour change; the first paint never animates.

### Listbox Roster (KQ-164) — `listbox-roster`
A marker glides the roster; ticks draw in.

An always-open multi-select — a roster you scan rather than a menu you poke. One highlight marker glides between rows as focus moves, ticking a row draws its check, and a running count keeps score.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/listbox-roster`

Props:
- `options`: ListOption[] — Each row has an id, a label, and an optional hint.
- `value / defaultValue`: string[] — Controlled or initial selection.
- `onValueChange`: (ids) => void — Fires the selected ids whenever they change.

Notes:
- Follows the listbox pattern on a roving tabindex — Up and Down walk and wrap, Home and End jump, Space or Enter toggles, under aria-multiselectable.
- The running count is announced politely.
- Under reduced motion the marker and checks are placed without the glide or draw.

### Tri Toggle (KQ-165) — `tri-toggle`
A switch with a considered middle.

A switch with a middle. One knob rides a three-stop track — off, a considered middle, on — gliding to the chosen stop on snap with a single crisp overshoot, keyed by a shared layoutId so the same knob travels rather than three knobs blinking.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/tri-toggle`

Props:
- `value / defaultValue`: string — Controlled or initial position.
- `options`: [TriOption, TriOption, TriOption] — Exactly three stops; defaults to off / auto / on.
- `name`: string — Renders a hidden native radio per stop so forms post the value.

Notes:
- A radio group under the hood — a roving tabindex where Left and Right step without wrapping past the ends, Home and End jump to off and on, Space selects.
- Exactly three positions; the knob glides between them on the snap spring.
- Under reduced motion the knob swaps to its stop instantly, colour only.

### Skeleton Weave (KQ-166) — `skeleton-weave`
The placeholder weaves, then hands over.

A placeholder that holds the shape of what is coming, then hands over. A sheen sweeps each row on a diagonal, staggered down the stack so the block reads as one woven surface; when the content arrives the placeholder fades and the real thing rises the last few pixels into place.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/skeleton-weave`

Props:
- `loading`: boolean — While true the placeholder holds the space.
- `rows`: string[] — Placeholder row widths; longest first reads like prose.
- `children`: ReactNode — The real content, revealed when loading turns false.

Notes:
- The placeholder is aria-hidden and the region is aria-busy, so assistive tech is told the state once rather than made to read decorative bars.
- A polite live region announces the wait.
- Under reduced motion the placeholder is a still surface and the content simply appears.

### Stage Progress (KQ-167) — `stage-progress`
See which stage is slow, not just a percent.

Progress that admits it has parts. Each stage owns a segment: the ones behind you are filled and ticked, the one in flight fills to its own fraction or breathes when it cannot say, and the ones ahead wait as empty track — so you can see which stage is slow.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/stage-progress`

Props:
- `stages`: Stage[] — The ordered stages; each owns a segment and a label.
- `current`: number — Index of the stage in flight; earlier ones have landed.
- `progress`: number — 0..1 within the current stage; omit for indeterminate.

Notes:
- Fills ride scaleX off a left origin rather than an animated width, so the bar never triggers layout.
- The strip reports as one progressbar whose aria-valuetext names the stage, not a percentage.
- Under reduced motion fills land in a frame, the tick is simply there, and the indeterminate stage holds steady.

### Alert Bar (KQ-168) — `alert-bar`
A notice that takes its own space, by severity.

An inline notice that takes its own space rather than floating over the work. Opening glides the row down from zero height while a severity stripe draws itself down the leading edge; dismissing collapses it back and the space closes behind it, so nothing below jumps.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/alert-bar`

Props:
- `open / defaultOpen`: boolean — Controlled or initial visibility.
- `severity`: "info" | "success" | "warn" | "danger" (default "info") — Sets the stripe, the title tone, and how loudly it speaks.
- `title / children`: string / ReactNode — The heading and an optional body.

Notes:
- Severity sets the role: warnings and failures mount as an assertive alert; info and success report politely and wait their turn.
- Colour is paired with a title tone, never asked to carry the meaning alone.
- Under reduced motion the row and its stripe are simply present or gone.

### Status Pip (KQ-169) — `status-pip`
Presence that breathes, and morphs its colour.

A presence dot that stays alive without shouting. Live states breathe a slow halo out and fade it; changing state morphs the dot's colour rather than cutting to it, and the status name always travels with the dot so colour is only ever a reinforcement.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/status-pip`

Props:
- `status`: "online" | "away" | "busy" | "offline" — The current state; sets the colour and the announced name.
- `label`: ReactNode — Visible text beside the dot; omit and the status name is read.
- `pulse`: boolean — Force the halo on or off; defaults on for live states.

Notes:
- Reports through a polite live region, so a change from Online to Busy is announced once.
- Colour is always paired with the status name, never the only cue.
- Under reduced motion the halo drops and the colour simply changes.

### Retry Pulse (KQ-170) — `retry-pulse`
Fail shakes, retry spins, success stamps.

An action that carries its own outcome. Failing snaps the button sideways once, then offers the retry; retrying spins a bearing where the label was; succeeding stamps a check that draws itself and lands on the recoil spring — all through one slot, so the control never changes width.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/retry-pulse`

Props:
- `status`: "idle" | "loading" | "success" | "error" — The controlled state driving the button.
- `onRetry`: () => void — Fired on activation while idle or errored.
- `label / retryLabel`: string (default "Submit" / "Retry") — The idle label and the errored label.

Notes:
- Reports aria-busy while it works and each outcome through a polite live region, so the state is spoken.
- The four states swap through one fixed slot, so the button never changes width under the cursor.
- Under reduced motion the shake, spin, and draw drop; the icons are simply there.

### Type On (KQ-171) — `type-on`
Types itself out, un-types on the swap.

A line that types itself out behind a caret and un-types when it is replaced. One motion value holds the revealed length and the shown substring is derived from it, so the whole run costs a single render however long the line; swapping the text backspaces the old and types the new.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/type-on`

Props:
- `text`: string — The line to type; changing it de-types then re-types.
- `speed`: number (default 26) — Characters per second.
- `as / caret`: "span" | "p" | "h2" | "h3" / boolean (default "p" / true) — The wrapper element and whether the caret blinks.

Notes:
- The full line is always present for assistive tech and search; only the animated copy is aria-hidden, so a screen reader is never fed one character at a time.
- The substring is derived off the motion value, so a long line never costs per-character renders.
- Under reduced motion the whole line prints at once with a steady caret.

### Highlight Sweep (KQ-172) — `highlight-sweep`
The marker sweeps in as the phrase arrives.

A highlighter that draws itself across a phrase the moment it scrolls into view. The marker is a wash behind the text scaled from a left origin, so it fills the way a pen would rather than fading in; it runs once, then holds.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/highlight-sweep`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The phrase to highlight.
- `tone`: "accent" | "success" | "warn" (default "accent") — The marker wash colour.

Notes:
- The text is the content and the wash is decoration, so nothing changes what a screen reader reads.
- The sweep runs once on view via an in-view trigger, then holds.
- Under reduced motion the wash is placed without the sweep.

### Gradient Title (KQ-173) — `gradient-title`
A headline whose sheen tracks your pointer.

A heading painted with a sheen that follows the pointer. The gradient is clipped to the glyphs and oversized, so moving the pointer only slides its position — one motion value, no re-render — while the weight leans heavier under your hand and eases back when you leave.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/gradient-title`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The heading text.
- `as`: "span" | "p" | "h1" | "h2" | "h3" (default "h2") — The semantic wrapper element.

Notes:
- Real text inside a real heading element — structure and search are untouched; the sheen is decoration.
- The sheen slides off one motion value, so a pointer move never costs a render.
- Reduced motion or a coarse pointer holds the sheen centred and the weight steady.

### Redact Reveal (KQ-174) — `redact-reveal`
The bar wipes back to reveal the words.

Text under a redaction bar that wipes away to show the words. The bar scales off its left edge, so the line is uncovered left-to-right like a marker pulled back rather than a fade; covering it again wipes the bar closed.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/redact-reveal`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The words under the bar.
- `revealed`: boolean — Controlled reveal; omit to let the trigger drive it.
- `revealOn`: "hover" | "press" (default "hover") — Uncover while hovered/focused, or toggle on a press.

Notes:
- The words underneath are always real text, readable to assistive tech whether or not the bar is drawn — the redaction is purely visual.
- In press mode the field is a real toggle button; in hover mode it uncovers on hover or focus.
- Under reduced motion the bar swaps in and out with no wipe.

### Balance Quote (KQ-175) — `balance-quote`
A balanced quote that rises word by word.

A pull-quote that sets itself well and arrives in order. The lines are balanced so no widow is left hanging, and on the way in the words rise and resolve one after another in a tight cascade — the eye is walked through the sentence rather than shown all of it at once.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/balance-quote`

Props:
- `children`: string — The quotation, as plain text.
- `cite`: string — Attribution shown beneath.

Notes:
- The whole quotation is one block of real text, so it reads as a sentence to assistive tech; only the per-word rise is decoration.
- The cascade runs once on view via an in-view trigger, then rests.
- Under reduced motion the quote is set in place with no cascade.

### Split Pane (KQ-176) — `split-pane`
Drag the divider; it snaps to thirds.

Two panes with a divider you can move. Dragging tracks the handle one to one; on release it eases to the nearest snap — the thirds and the half — if it is close, and stays put otherwise. The grabber swells while hovered or held so the hit target announces itself.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/split-pane`

Props:
- `start / end`: ReactNode — The two pane contents.
- `defaultSplit / min / max`: number (default 50 / 20 / 80) — Start width and travel limits, in percent.
- `snap`: number[] — Targets the divider settles to on release, within 4%.

Notes:
- The divider is a real separator with a value — arrows nudge, Shift jumps, Home and End go to the limits, and it reads its position aloud.
- Drag is one to one; the snap glides only on release.
- Under reduced motion the snap lands without the glide.

### Masonry Flow (KQ-177) — `masonry-flow`
Filter the wall; the tiles FLIP to fit.

A masonry that keeps its tiles when the set changes. Columns are laid by the browser so the count follows the width for free; when a filter drops or adds tiles, each survivor FLIPs from its old box to its new one while the ones leaving fade down and the ones arriving fade up.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/masonry-flow`

Props:
- `items`: MasonryItem[] — Tiles keyed by a stable id so motion tracks them across reflows.
- `minColumnWidth`: string (default "10rem") — Column width; the browser fits as many as span.

Notes:
- Tiles are keyed by a stable id, which lets motion follow the same tile across a reflow rather than cross-fading unrelated boxes.
- The parent controls the filtered set; the component animates whatever it is handed.
- Under reduced motion the FLIP and fades drop; tiles appear in their new places.

### Expander Tree (KQ-178) — `expander-tree`
Open a branch; the children cascade in.
