A nested disclosure that opens with a little procession. Expanding a node glides its panel to height while a guide line draws down the indent and the children arrive one after another, so the hierarchy reads as it unfolds rather than snapping open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/expander-tree`

Props:
- `nodes`: TreeNode[] — The tree; each node has a label and optional children.
- `defaultExpanded`: string[] — Node ids expanded on first render.

Notes:
- Each parent is a real disclosure button carrying aria-expanded over the region it controls, so the tree is walked and toggled with Tab and Space.
- The guide line and the per-child cascade are decorative; the structure is the list.
- Under reduced motion panels open and close instantly with no draw or cascade.

### Sticky Stack (KQ-179) — `sticky-stack`
Headers stack at the top, then peel away.

Scroll a run of sections and their headers stack up at the top instead of one replacing the next. Each header sticks a header-height lower than the last, piling into a legible ledger of where you are; when a section scrolls past, its header releases and peels away, uncovering the one beneath.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/sticky-stack`

Props:
- `sections`: StackSection[] — Each carries a title and its content.
- `headerHeight / height`: number (default 44 / 320) — Header height (the stack step) and the viewport height, in px.

Notes:
- Pure sticky positioning — no scroll listeners, no transforms, nothing per frame — so it is inert under reduced motion and cheap on a long list.
- The scroll region is a labelled, keyboard-focusable landmark.
- Headers stack by an increasing sticky offset, then peel with their section when it ends.

### Cursor Lens (KQ-180) — `cursor-lens`
A glass that magnifies under your pointer.

A round glass that follows the pointer and magnifies whatever is beneath it. The magnified copy scales about the exact point under the cursor and a fixed circular clip travels with it, both driven off two motion values — so the glass tracks the cursor without a single render.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/cursor-lens`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The content under the glass; keep it presentational.
- `zoom / size`: number (default 2 / 128) — Magnification and lens diameter in px.

Notes:
- The lens tracks the pointer off motion values, so moving it never costs a render.
- A fine-pointer instrument: on touch it steps aside and the content is left plain; the magnified copy is aria-hidden, so it is read once.
- It has no ambient motion — it moves only when you do — so it needs no reduced-motion pathway.

### Cursor Label (KQ-181) — `cursor-label`
The cursor names what it is over.

A cursor that says what a thing does. Inside the region it rides a small dot that trails the pointer; cross an element tagged with a verb and the dot morphs into a pill carrying that word, snapping back to a dot on the way out — the guidance lives with the element.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/cursor-label`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The region; tag any element with data-cursor="Verb".

Notes:
- The pill is decoration and aria-hidden; the real elements keep their own roles, focus, and hit areas.
- A fine-pointer flourish — on touch the region renders plain.
- Under reduced motion the pill tracks and morphs without the trailing spring.

### Trail Ink (KQ-182) — `trail-ink`
Ink that follows your hand, then dries.

A brush that lays wet ink under the pointer and lets it dry away. Each sample is stamped with a birth time; the loop tapers every segment by how fast you were moving and fades it as it ages, so a fast flick leaves a thin quick line and a slow drag a heavier one, then the whole stroke dries to nothing.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/trail-ink`

Props:
- `height`: number (default 260) — Panel height in px.
- `fade`: number (default 1.4) — Seconds a stroke takes to dry and fade.

Notes:
- A disciplined canvas: DPR capped at 2, ResizeObserver, IntersectionObserver + visibility gating, and it idle-stops the moment the last stroke has dried.
- Colours are read from the theme and re-resolved on a light/dark flip.
- Under reduced motion there is no drying loop — the pointer leaves a still mark and moves on.

### Reveal Stagger (KQ-183) — `reveal-stagger`
Children rise into view, one after another.

Wrap a group and its children arrive in sequence as it scrolls into view — each rising a little and resolving, stepped by a stagger that tightens automatically so a long list never reads as lag. It runs once, then stays put.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/reveal-stagger`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The group; each direct child gets its own entrance.
- `step`: number — Seconds between children; auto-tightens under the budget.

Notes:
- Children are rendered exactly as given, each in its own layer, so markup and reading order are unchanged.
- The entrance runs once on view via an in-view trigger.
- Under reduced motion every child is placed at once with no rise.

### Marquee Swap (KQ-184) — `marquee-swap`
Lines roll upward through one slot.

One line at a time, rolling upward. The current line lifts out the top as the next rises from the bottom into the same clipped slot, so a run of phrases reads as a single rolling display rather than a stack.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/marquee-swap`

Props:
- `items`: string[] — The lines to roll through, in order.
- `interval`: number (default 2.6) — Seconds each line holds before the next rolls up.

Notes:
- The live line is announced politely, so the rotation is not lost to a screen reader.
- The slot is clipped, so only the live line shows.
- Reduced motion is deliberately still — it holds the first line and does not auto-roll.

### Progress Scrub (KQ-185) — `progress-scrub`
A progress bar you can also scrub.

A reading bar that both reports and drives. As you scroll the region its fill tracks how far through you are; grab the bar and you scrub the other way — the region scrolls to wherever you drag, so the same control shows progress and seeks with it.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/progress-scrub`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The scrollable content.
- `height`: number (default 240) — Scroll region height in px.

Notes:
- The bar is a real slider — it carries its position for assistive tech, and arrows, Home, and End step and jump the scroll.
- Scrolling and scrubbing are two-way bound onto the same control.
- Under reduced motion a seek jumps straight to the target instead of gliding.

### Sticky Reveal (KQ-186) — `sticky-reveal`
The stage pins; scenes cross-fade on scroll.

A stage that stays put while the story scrolls through it. The panel pins to the top and the scroll drives which scene is lit — each cross-fades in as its stretch of the track arrives while a rail of dots marks the position, so a short sequence plays out in place.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/sticky-reveal`

Props:
- `scenes`: Scene[] — The ordered scenes, one lit at a time.
- `height`: number (default 300) — Pinned stage height in px.

Notes:
- The scroll region is a labelled, keyboard-focusable landmark, and off-scene panels are hidden from assistive tech.
- Reduced motion refuses the pin: it lays the scenes out as a plain stacked list, all readable.
- Nothing is gated behind the scroll animation.

### Confetti Pop (KQ-187) — `confetti-pop`
A press throws confetti that rains back down.

A burst of confetti with real fall to it. Each bit is thrown from the press point with a seeded spread, then pulled down by gravity, slowed by drag, spun, and faded — so the spray arcs up and rains back. Colours come from the theme, and it is deterministic: the same press throws the same shape.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/confetti-pop`

Props:
- `count`: number (default 70) — Bits per burst.
- `onPop`: () => void — Fires on each burst.
- `height`: number (default 220) — Panel height in px.

Notes:
- The canvas is DPR-capped, ResizeObserver-sized, IntersectionObserver + visibility gated, and idle-stops the instant the last bit has fallen.
- The trigger is a real button (Enter and Space); the canvas is decorative and aria-hidden.
- Under reduced motion a press lays a single still spray instead of raining.

### Sound Toggle (KQ-188) — `sound-toggle`
The waves draw out; the slash strikes in.

A mute switch whose sound is visible. Turning it on draws the waves out of the speaker in a quick outward stagger; muting retracts them and strikes a slash across in their place, so the state is legible at a glance without reading a word.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/sound-toggle`

Props:
- `on / defaultOn`: boolean — Controlled or initial sound-on state.
- `onChange`: (on) => void — Fires the new state on toggle.

Notes:
- A real switch — checked and labelled, toggles on Enter and Space, state announced.
- The waves and slash carry the state visually so colour is never the only cue.
- Under reduced motion the waves and slash swap in place with no draw.

### Sticker Peel (KQ-189) — `sticker-peel`
Grab a corner; it curls, then lifts clean off.

A sticker you can actually peel. Drag it and the grabbed corner curls up off the surface, a shadow pools beneath, and the card tilts away; release before the threshold and it snaps back into place, or past it and it lifts clean off to bare the dashed slot underneath. A paired button peels and re-sticks it for keyboard and pointer-free use.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/sticker-peel`

Props:
- `children`: ReactNode — The sticker face.
- `width / height`: number — Sticker size in px.
- `threshold`: number — Drag distance in px past which a release removes it.
- `label`: string — Accessible name for the sticker and its control.
- `onPeel / onRestore`: () => void — Fire when the sticker lifts off or re-sticks.

Notes:
- Drag any corner to peel; release short of the threshold and it recoils back into the slot.
- The paired button peels and re-sticks it, so the interaction is never drag-only.
- Under reduced motion the lift is a plain fade with no curl, tilt, or spring.

### Boop Mascot (KQ-190) — `boop-mascot`
Boop it — the whole head squashes and springs back.

A small face that reacts. Its eyes follow the pointer on a drift spring, and a click, tap, or Enter/Space boops it — the whole head squashes and springs back, the eyes pinch shut, the smile rounds into an open O, and the cheeks flush for a beat before it settles.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/boop-mascot`

Props:
- `label`: string — Accessible name for the toy.
- `size`: number — Rendered pixel size of the square face.
- `onBoop`: () => void — Fires on every boop.

Notes:
- A real button — boops on click, tap, and Enter or Space, with an accessible name.
- Eye-tracking only engages for a fine pointer; touch just boops.
- Under reduced motion it still boops the face and cheeks but skips the squash and the eye spring.

### Popover Menu (KQ-191) — `popover-menu`
Unfolds from the trigger; the arrow keeps its aim.

A trigger-anchored menu that unfolds from its button on a single crisp overshoot, a small arrow tracking the trigger. It flips side or alignment when the viewport runs short so it never spills off-screen, and carries full menu semantics: roving arrow keys, Home and End, Escape and outside-click to close, and focus returned to the trigger on select.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/popover-menu`

Props:
- `items`: PopoverMenuItem[] — The menu rows — id, label, optional icon, disabled, onSelect.
- `children`: ReactNode — Trigger label / content.
- `side`: top | bottom — Preferred side; flips when the viewport is tight.
- `align`: start | end — Preferred horizontal edge; flips on overflow.
- `label`: string — Accessible name for the menu surface.

Notes:
- Open with click or ArrowDown; move with the arrow keys, Home, and End; select with Enter.
- The panel flips side and alignment to stay on-screen, and the arrow re-aims at the trigger.
- Escape or an outside click closes it and returns focus to the trigger.
- Under reduced motion it appears without the unfold or the row cascade.

### Context Menu (KQ-192) — `context-menu`
Springs from the right-click; cascades into submenus.

A menu that springs from the point you right-click. It clamps to the viewport — flipping up or left near an edge — cascades its rows in, and opens nested submenus to the side on hover or ArrowRight. Full keyboard control: the Menu or Shift+F10 key opens it from the focused region, arrows and Home/End move, ArrowLeft steps back out of a submenu, Escape and an outside click dismiss, and focus returns to the region.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/context-menu`

Props:
- `items`: ContextMenuItem[] — Rows — id, label, icon, shortcut, disabled, separator, nested items, onSelect.
- `children`: ReactNode — The region that owns the menu.
- `label`: string — Accessible name for the menu surface.

Notes:
- Right-click the region, or focus it and press the Menu key or Shift+F10.
- Arrows and Home/End move; ArrowRight opens a submenu, ArrowLeft steps back out.
- Escape or an outside click closes it and returns focus to the region.
- Under reduced motion it appears without the spring or the row cascade.

### Hover Card (KQ-193) — `hover-card`
A preview that floats up from an inline anchor.

A rich preview that expands from an inline anchor. Hovering the trigger — or focusing it — floats a card in on a single overshoot with a small arrow; the pointer can cross into the card without it closing, and it retracts a beat after you leave. The card flips above the anchor when the space below runs short.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/hover-card`

Props:
- `trigger`: ReactNode — Inline anchor — a link, name, or chip.
- `children`: ReactNode — Rich card content; may be interactive.
- `side / align`: top | bottom / start | center | end — Preferred placement; side flips when the viewport is tight.
- `openDelay / closeDelay`: number — Hover open delay and the grace period before closing, in ms.

Notes:
- Opens on hover after a short delay, or immediately on keyboard focus.
- The pointer can move onto the card to interact without it closing.
- Escape closes it; the card flips above the anchor when the space below is short.
- Under reduced motion it cross-fades in place with no scale or travel.

### Spotlight Tour (KQ-194) — `spotlight-tour`
A moving spotlight walks the eye through each step.

A guided onboarding tour. A dimming scrim cuts a moving spotlight over each target in turn while a popover explains it, gliding from one step to the next; the highlighted element stays lit through the dark. Arrow keys and the buttons step through, Escape or the close control ends it, focus is trapped in the popover, and the spotlight re-tracks its target on scroll and resize.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spotlight-tour`

Props:
- `steps`: TourStep[] — Ordered steps — id, a target ref, title, and body.
- `open`: boolean — Whether the tour is running (controlled).
- `onOpenChange`: (open) => void — Fires when the tour opens or closes.
- `onFinish`: () => void — Fires when the last step is completed, not merely dismissed.

Notes:
- Point each step at an element via a ref; the spotlight scrolls it into view.
- Arrow keys move between steps, Escape or the close button ends the tour.
- Focus is trapped in the popover and the scrim traps clicks behind it.
- Under reduced motion the spotlight jumps between steps instead of gliding.

### Command Deck (KB-101) — `command-deck`
A palette that arms before it fires.

A command palette that arms before it fires — a deliberate fill sweeps the selected row before execution, so destructive commands never feel accidental.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/command-deck`

Props:
- `commands`: DeckCommand[] — { id, label, hint?, icon?, section?, keywords?, destructive?, run } per command.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled visibility.
- `hotkey`: boolean (default true) — Global ⌘K / Ctrl+K toggle listener.
- `inline / portal`: boolean (default false / true) — Inline renders just the panel (docs/demos); portal overlays into body with focus trap and scroll lock.
- `recentKey`: string (default "kinetiq-deck-recents") — localStorage key for the five most recent command ids.

Notes:
- Full combobox semantics with focus trap and restore; Escape mid-arm cancels the sweep.
- Under reduced motion the arming sweep becomes an instant fill with a deliberate 120ms pause.

### Access Panel (KB-102) — `access-panel`
Sign-in that unlocks like a vault.

Sign-in that unlocks like a vault — steps ride a gantry rail, OTP digits drop into their cells, and the right code throws a breaker bolt open.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/access-panel`

Props:
- `expectedCode`: string (default "246810") — The six-digit code that throws the bolt.
- `onVerify`: (code: string) => void — Fires with the entered code on every attempt.
- `onComplete`: (email: string) => void — Fires once when the correct code lands.
- `email`: string — Prefills the email field.

Notes:
- One hidden input with autocomplete="one-time-code" drives the six visual cells — the accessibility-correct OTP pattern.
- Under reduced motion the rail slides become fades and the rejection nudge is skipped; the alert still announces.

### Signal Center (KB-103) — `signal-center`
An inbox that files itself.

An inbox that files itself — signals arrive on the conveyor, swipe away with real velocity, and clear-all sweeps the floor in a cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/signal-center`

Props:
- `signals / defaultSignals / onSignalsChange`: Signal[] / Signal[] / (signals) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled feed; Signal = { id, source, title, detail?, time, read? }.
- `onArchive`: (signal: Signal) => void — Fires per archived signal, including clear-all.
- `title`: string (default "Signals") — Header title next to the live unread readout.

Notes:
- Archiving is keyboard-reachable via a per-row button; swiping is the fast path, not the only path.
- Under reduced motion arrivals and exits become fades and clear-all loses its stagger.

### Media Console (KB-104) — `media-console`
A media island that unfolds into a console.

A media island that unfolds into a console — the pill morphs open into transport, a caliper timeline, and a scope-scrubbed volume.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/media-console`

Props:
- `tracks`: Track[] — { id, title, artist, duration } — playback is simulated; the JSDoc shows wiring a real audio element.
- `defaultExpanded`: boolean (default false) — Start as the pill or the full console.

Notes:
- Complete keyboard transport; Escape collapses the console back to the pill.
- Under reduced motion the morph becomes a crossfade and the waveform holds still.

### Checkout Receipt (KB-105) — `checkout-receipt`
Payment that prints its proof.

Payment that prints its proof — hold to pay, then a receipt feeds out of the slot line by line, tears off, and takes a PAID stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/checkout-receipt`

Props:
- `items`: ReceiptItem[] — Line items ({ name, price }) summed into the total.
- `currency`: string (default "$") — Price prefix.
- `onPay`: () => void — Fires when the hold completes.

Notes:
- The receipt is real text in a definition list; only the barcode is decorative.
- Under reduced motion the receipt appears whole and the stamp fades in.

### Intake Tray (KB-106) — `intake-tray`
Files land, physically.

Files land, physically — dropped files fall into the tray with a squash, shingle into a stack, fill their gauge rings, and slide to the processed rail.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/intake-tray`

Props:
- `onFiles`: (files: File[]) => void — Receives each accepted batch.
- `accept / maxFiles`: string / number (default — / 6) — Accepted types hint and tray capacity.
- `simulate`: boolean (default true) — Hash-driven demo progress; the JSDoc documents wiring real uploads.
- `ref`: Ref<IntakeTrayHandle> — Imperative addFiles() for programmatic intake.

Notes:
- The drop zone is a real button with a labeled native file input — fully keyboard operable.
- Under reduced motion chips stack neatly with fades; the gauge rings still fill.

### Readout Grid (KB-107) — `readout-grid`
Dashboard stats as instrument cards.

Dashboard stats as instrument cards — a carry-rolling counter, a self-drawing sparkline, a split-flap delta, and a needle gauge, mounted with one cascade.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/readout-grid`

Props:
- `metrics`: ReadoutGridMetrics — Optional full override of the four cards: counter, sparkline, flap, and gauge shapes.

Notes:
- Each card is an article whose label reads naturally to screen readers; chart visuals carry sr-only values.
- Under reduced motion cards render in place, the sparkline arrives fully drawn, and the needle jumps.

### Launch Checklist (KB-108) — `launch-checklist`
Setup steps that stamp themselves done.

Onboarding checklist where ticks draw themselves, finished steps strike through and settle to the bottom, and completing the set lands a CALIBRATED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/launch-checklist`

Props:
- `steps`: ChecklistStep[] — Step definitions: id, title, optional description.
- `completed / defaultCompleted`: string[] — Controlled or uncontrolled set of completed step ids.
- `onCompletedChange`: (completed: string[]) => void — Fires on every toggle with the new completed set.
- `onComplete`: () => void — Fires once when the final step completes.

Notes:
- Rows are native checkboxes under the hood — the whole block is keyboard and screen-reader operable.
- Under reduced motion rows reorder instantly and the stamp fades in; the progress track still reports value via ARIA.

### Iris Menu (KB-109) — `iris-menu`
Actions bloom from where you pressed.

Actions bloom from where you pressed — items launch from the trigger's center to seats along the freest quadrant's arc, and fold back in on close.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/iris-menu`

Props:
- `items`: IrisMenuItem[] — Up to six: { id, icon, label, onSelect, disabled?, destructive? }.
- `placement`: "auto" | quadrant (default "auto") — Auto measures the viewport and blooms into the roomiest quadrant.
- `radius`: number (default 84) — Distance from trigger center to each seat.

Notes:
- Arrow keys rotate focus around the ring; Escape closes and restores the trigger.
- Under reduced motion items fade in already seated — no travel.

### Overflow Rail (KB-110) — `overflow-rail`
More actions, sprung from the dots.

More actions, sprung from the dots — the pill rail morphs open inline while primaries yield with a recoil part and secondaries cascade in.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/overflow-rail`

Props:
- `primary / secondary`: RailAction[] — Always-visible actions and the set revealed by the ⋯ trigger.
- `open / defaultOpen / onOpenChange`: boolean / boolean / (open) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled expansion.
- `label`: string (default "Actions") — Accessible toolbar name.

Notes:
- One toolbar: arrows rove across every visible action; Escape collapses and refocuses the trigger.
- Under reduced motion the width jumps and secondaries fade in place.

### Beacon (KB-111) — `beacon`
One capsule, every live activity.

One capsule, every live activity — the shell morphs between timer, upload, call, and now-playing views with blur crossfades, corners pinned.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/beacon`

Props:
- `activity`: BeaconActivity | null — Discriminated union: timer (endsAt), upload (progress), call (accept/decline), playing (title/artist); null collapses to a standby pill.
- `expanded / defaultExpanded / onExpandedChange`: boolean / boolean / (expanded) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled detail row.
- `onDismiss`: () => void — Renders a dismiss action in the detail row.

Notes:
- Call buttons are reachable while collapsed; activity changes announce once, debounced.
- Under reduced motion the shell resizes instantly and the loops hold still.

### Field Report (KB-112) — `field-report`
Feedback that files itself.

Feedback that files itself — a notch rating with a gliding indicator, a growing note field, and a submit that folds the form into a slot under a LOGGED stamp.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/field-report`

Props:
- `onSubmit`: ({ rating, note }) => void — Fires on valid submit; rating is 1–5.
- `prompt / endLabels`: string / [string, string] (default "How did it feel?" / ["Rough","Dialed"]) — The question and the rail's end labels.
- `resetAfterMs`: number — Fades a fresh form back in after filing.

Notes:
- The rating is a real radiogroup — arrows move the indicator, and empty submits nudge with a role=alert line.
- Under reduced motion the filing collapse becomes a fade and the stamp lands statically.

### Not Found (KB-113) — `not-found`
Sweep complete. Sector empty.

Sweep complete, sector empty — a radar arc scans a quiet grid while the 404 numeral deciphers itself, with home and command-deck exits.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/not-found`

Props:
- `homeHref`: string (default "/") — Return-to-base link target.
- `onCommandDeck`: () => void — Renders the ghost command-deck action when provided.
- `code / message`: string (default "404" / sector message) — The cipher numeral and the rising subline.

Notes:
- The heading carries real text for screen readers; the cipher layer and radar are decorative.
- Under reduced motion the sweep freezes mid-arc and the numeral renders plainly.

### Forecast Card (KB-114) — `forecast-card`
Every vote moves every bar.

Every vote moves every bar — one commit renormalizes the whole field while percentages carry-roll and the leader tick migrates to the new front-runner.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/forecast-card`

Props:
- `question / options`: string / ForecastOption[] — 2–4 outcomes as { id, label, votes }; options are always the source of counts.
- `votedId / defaultVotedId / onVote`: string | null / string / (id) => void — Controlled or uncontrolled viewer vote.
- `allowRevote`: boolean (default false) — Let the viewer move their vote between outcomes.
- `closesAt`: string | Date — Compact mono CLOSES footer line.

Notes:
- Rows are aria-pressed buttons; each vote announces the new percentage and leadership once.
- Under reduced motion widths jump and the leader tick teleports; the readouts pulse instead of rolling.

### Balance Card (KB-115) — `balance-card`
A balance with a private side.

A balance with a private side — the numeral carry-rolls behind a privacy blur, a sparkline draws itself, and the card flips to its activity face.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/balance-card`

Props:
- `balance / format / delta / series`: number / (v)=>string / {value,direction} / number[] — The carry-rolling amount, its delta chip, and the sparkline data.
- `activity`: BalanceActivity[] — Back-face rows: { id, label, amount, time }.
- `onAction`: (action: "send" | "receive" | "convert") => void — Fires from the action row.
- `defaultHidden`: boolean (default false) — Start with the balance blurred.

Notes:
- The hidden face is inert and aria-hidden — the flip never traps focus or leaks taps.
- Under reduced motion the flip is instant, the sparkline arrives drawn, and the blur toggles without a tween.

### Exchange Panel (KB-116) — `exchange-panel`
Two units, one clean swap.

Two units, one clean swap — the edited side is live, the computed side carry-rolls after a debounce, and swapping keeps focus while contents trade places.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/exchange-panel`

Props:
- `units`: ExchangeUnit[] | ExchangeGroup[] — Linear factors to a shared base unit; groups render a grouped select.
- `feeRate`: number (default 0) — Fraction applied to the converted side, shown as a fee row.
- `onChange / onDirectionChange`: (state) => void / (from, to) => void — Conversion updates and swap events.
- `debounceMs`: number (default 200) — Delay before the computed side rolls.

Notes:
- One side holds authority; the other is always derived — swapping transfers authority with the gesture and refocuses the input.
- Under reduced motion contents swap instantly and the readout uses its highlight fallback.

### Utils — `utils`
cn() — class composition.

Class-name composition helper shared by every component.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/utils`

### Calibration Set — `motion`
flick · snap · glide · drift · recoil.

Kinetiq's five calibrated springs plus the tween scale, cascade budget, and exit rules — the shared physics vocabulary every component draws from.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/motion`

### useMotionSafe — `use-motion-safe`
Reduced motion, decided once.

Single source of truth for reduced-motion decisions: combines the OS preference with an optional app-level override context.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-motion-safe`

### Spatial Set — `spatial`
Perspective, detents, orbits, lift.

The shared geometry vocabulary of the spatial instruments — house perspective range, angle detents, contact-lift shadows, orbit projection, and deterministic seeding.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/spatial`

### usePointerTilt — `use-pointer-tilt`
Pointer → sprung perspective.

Normalized pointer tracking mapped to sprung tilt values — the house idiom for pointer-driven perspective, with an underdamped rebalance on leave and a fine-pointer gate.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/use-pointer-tilt`

### Agent Rules — `agents-rules`
The design-system operating rules for agents.

Kinetiq's operating rules for coding agents: the five-spring motion language, the tween scale, the 600ms cascade budget, the reduced-motion policy, token usage, and composition guidance. Installs as AGENTS.md at your repo root so your agent stays on the system's vocabulary.

Install: `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest add @kinetiq/agents-rules`
